home.seoDesc: Montenegro's beach capital has 17 beaches and 33% of all tourism. Grab a car from €13/day and drive the riviera from Jaz to Sveti Stefan. Tivat Airport pickup, 22 km away.

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Helping travellers explore the Budva Riviera and beyond.
about.seoDesc: Riviera specialists who plan beach-to-mountain itineraries daily. We know which beach car parks fill first and which coast roads avoid the summer queues.
about.p1: Budva Car Hire connects travellers with reliable, affordable rental cars in Budva and across the Montenegrin Riviera. Our mission is simple: making it easy for visitors to explore this stunning stretch of Adriatic coastline on their own terms.
about.p2: Budva is Montenegro's top tourist destination, welcoming over 800,000 visitors a year. Its 2,500-year-old Old Town, world-class beaches like Mogren and Jaz, and proximity to Sveti Stefan make it the perfect base for a road trip. But public transport only gets you so far — a rental car unlocks the entire riviera.
about.p3a: We provide car rental services throughout
about.p3d: (65 km), and locations across the coast and interior.
about.promise1: Full insurance with zero excess options available
about.promise2: Tivat Airport meet-and-greet — we bring the car to you at arrivals
about.promise3: No hidden fees — the price you see is the price you pay
about.promise4: Cross-border travel permitted to Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, Serbia, and Kosovo
about.localText: Based on the Budva Riviera, we know the roads, the best routes, and the hidden gems that guidebooks miss. Whether you're driving from
about.localMid2: Old Town, heading south to Sveti Stefan, or north to
about.localEnd: and the Bay of Kotor, we can help you plan the perfect road trip.
about.contactText: Have a question about renting a car in Budva? We're available via phone, WhatsApp, and Viber. Search our fleet online and book in minutes — or reach out and we'll help you find the perfect car for your trip.

kotor.subtitle: A 25 km drive to Montenegro's UNESCO jewel.
kotor.seoDesc: Head 25 km north along the scenic coastal road from beach resort to UNESCO fortress town. Kotor's medieval walls and bay views are half an hour from your sunbed.

tivat-airport.title: Tivat Airport Car Hire
tivat-airport.subtitle: Collect your rental car at arrivals — 22 km from Budva.
tivat-airport.seoDesc: 22 km and 30 minutes separate Tivat Airport from Budva's beaches. Your car waits at TIV arrivals — drive straight to the riviera without transfer hassle.

budva.title: Car Hire in Budva
budva.subtitle: Montenegro's riviera capital — beaches, nightlife, and 2,500 years of history.
budva.seoDesc: Old Town walls, the Citadela fortress, Mogren cave beach, and Top Hill — Europe's cliffside superclub. All within 5 km of your Budva pickup point.

perast.title: Perast from Budva
perast.subtitle: A baroque gem on the Bay of Kotor, 35 km from Budva.
perast.seoDesc: 40 minutes via the bay road swaps riviera sand for baroque stone. Perast's 16 churches and Our Lady of the Rocks boat trip make a perfect Budva day out.

podgorica.title: Podgorica from Budva
podgorica.subtitle: Montenegro's capital — 65 km from the riviera.
podgorica.seoDesc: 65 km inland from the beach to the capital. Pass Lake Skadar en route — the Balkans' largest lake with 270 bird species. An hour from sand to cityscape.

podgorica-airport.title: Podgorica Airport Car Hire
podgorica-airport.subtitle: Montenegro's main international gateway, 65 km from Budva.
podgorica-airport.seoDesc: Budget flights land at TGD — an hour's drive puts you on Budva's beaches. Collect at Podgorica arrivals and take the Lake Skadar route to the coast.

dubrovnik-airport.title: Dubrovnik Airport Car Hire
dubrovnik-airport.subtitle: Fly into Croatia, drive to Budva.
dubrovnik-airport.seoDesc: The coastal route from Dubrovnik Airport to Budva takes 2.5 hours with one border stop. Green card sorted — just enjoy the Adriatic views south.

tivat.title: Tivat from Budva
tivat.subtitle: Montenegro's marina town, 22 km from Budva.
tivat.seoDesc: Porto Montenegro's luxury marina is 30 minutes from Budva's party strip — superyachts, waterfront dining, and a different side of the coast entirely.

crossBorder.title: Cross-Border
crossBorder.desc: Drive into Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, or Kosovo. Cross-border travel permitted.

0.text: Collected a clean Octavia at Tivat Airport, drove straight to Budva in 20 minutes. Explored the whole riviera from Jaz Beach to Petrovac. Great service.

1.text: Stayed in Budva and rented a car for the week. Made it to Sveti Stefan, Kotor, and Lovcen. The cross-border paperwork for Croatia was included. Highly recommend.

2.text: The Jeep was perfect for the mountain roads above Budva. We drove up to Lovcen and down to Petrovac every day. Best holiday car hire we've used.

0.a: A valid driving licence (must show Latin script details), a foreign passport, and a physical credit card in the main driver's name. International Driving Permit recommended if your licence is only valid in its country of origin. Copies or electronic licences are not accepted.

1.q: Can I drive to Croatia or Albania?
1.a: Yes! If you plan to travel outside Montenegro, notify the rental agent in advance so they can complete the required documents. Cross-border travel to neighbouring countries is permitted.

2.a: The minimum age and driving experience requirements vary by vehicle. These details are shown when you search for available cars.

3.a: All vehicles come with insurance — most include Third Party Liability and Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) in the base price. VAT, equipment kits (first aid, warning triangle, visibility vest), and a transparent fuel policy are also included. Mileage terms vary by vehicle. Extended coverage options are available for an additional fee.

4.a: Choose your pickup time and location during booking. For Tivat Airport, we meet you at arrivals — Budva is a 22 km drive. For Budva pickups, the agent will arrange to meet you at your hotel or an agreed location.

5.q: Can I drop off at a different location?
5.a: Yes — one-way rentals are supported across 28 cities and towns in Montenegro. Simply choose different pickup and dropoff locations during booking.

6.a: Free cancellation up to 7 days before the rental start date. For luxury cars and convertibles, free cancellation is available up to 30 days before. A 6% payment service fee applies to all cancellations.

7.a: Deposit amounts vary by vehicle — some cars are available with no deposit. If required, the deposit must be on a physical credit card in the main driver's name and is refunded upon return of the vehicle in good condition.

8.q: What happens if I exceed the mileage limit?
8.a: Vehicles with mileage restrictions are charged per kilometre over the limit. Rates are shown in the vehicle details before you book.

9.a: No. The price you see includes all taxes and fees. What's shown at booking is what you pay.

10.q: How much do I pay upfront?
10.a: Only 15–20% of the total is required to confirm your booking. The remainder is paid directly to the rental agent at pickup.

11.q: Can I rent without a credit card?
11.a: Some vehicles are available without a credit card. Check availability when searching — card-free options are clearly marked.

aboutBody.p1: Budva Car Hire connects travellers with reliable, affordable rental cars in Budva and across the Montenegrin Riviera. Our mission is simple: making it easy for visitors to explore this stunning stretch of Adriatic coastline on their own terms.
aboutBody.p2: Budva is Montenegro's top tourist destination, welcoming over 800,000 visitors a year. Its 2,500-year-old Old Town, world-class beaches like Mogren and Jaz, and proximity to Sveti Stefan make it the perfect base for a road trip. But public transport only gets you so far — a rental car unlocks the entire riviera.
aboutBody.p3: We provide car rental services throughout the Budva Riviera and wider Montenegro, with pickup and dropoff at Tivat Airport (22 km), Podgorica Airport (65 km), and locations across the coast and interior. Our fleet ranges from compact city cars to spacious SUVs, all fully insured with no hidden fees.
aboutBody.promise1: Full insurance with zero excess options available
aboutBody.promise2: Tivat Airport meet-and-greet — we bring the car to you at arrivals
aboutBody.promise3: No hidden fees — the price you see is the price you pay
aboutBody.promise4: Cross-border travel permitted to Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, Serbia, and Kosovo
aboutBody.p4: Based on the Budva Riviera, we know the roads, the best routes, and the hidden gems that guidebooks miss. Whether you're driving from Tivat Airport to Budva's Old Town, heading south to Sveti Stefan and Petrovac, or north to Kotor and Perast, we can help you plan the perfect road trip.
aboutBody.p5: Have a question about renting a car in Budva? We're available via phone, WhatsApp, and Viber. Search our fleet online and book in minutes — or reach out and we'll help you find the perfect car for your trip.

kotorBody.p1: Kotor is one of Montenegro's most stunning seaside towns, offering historical Old Town charm, medieval walls housing art galleries, cafes, and excellent restaurants. As a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the town's landmarks are concentrated in the beautifully preserved Old Town.
kotorBody.p2: The drive from Budva to Kotor takes about 30 minutes through a dramatic mountain tunnel. Renting a car gives you the freedom to explore Kotor and continue along the Bay to Perast, Risan, and beyond at your own pace.
kotorBody.stJohnText: An imposing structure that historically protected the city from pirate attacks. Notable features include the Contarini Tower and the Our Lady of Health Church, which offers breathtaking views over the Old Town and the Bay of Kotor.
kotorBody.cathedralText: Built in 809 AD and reconstructed in 1124, this cathedral contains remarkable Byzantine architecture and art reflecting the region's rich history.
kotorBody.perastTrip: From Kotor, continue north along the bay to Perast (15 min), home to Our Lady of the Rocks island. Fishermen have dropped rocks there annually since 1452. Ferry access costs around 1 Euro.
kotorBody.cetinjeTrip: Head inland from Kotor via the famous 25 hairpin bends to Cetinje, the old royal capital. The monastery houses the hand of John the Baptist.
kotorBody.royalCourtText: Montenegro's royal palace from 1871, displaying art, artefacts, and photographs from the country's royal history. Located next to the monastery in the former royal capital.

budvaBody.h1: Budva — The Heart of the Montenegrin Riviera
budvaBody.p1: Budva is Montenegro's number one tourist destination, attracting over 800,000 visitors a year to its stunning coastline, vibrant nightlife, and beautifully preserved medieval Old Town. With a rental car, the entire Budva Riviera and beyond is at your fingertips.
budvaBody.p2: The town sits just 22 km from Tivat Airport — a 30-minute drive. Budva combines 2,500 years of history with a thoroughly modern holiday atmosphere. Ancient city walls by day, Top Hill club by night. Bečici beach was once voted the finest in Europe.
budvaBody.beachesIntro: The Budva Riviera stretches for 21 kilometres, offering some of the finest beaches on the Adriatic coast:
budvaBody.mogren: Two connected pebble beaches reached through a tunnel cut into the cliffs, just minutes from the Old Town
budvaBody.jaz: A large sandy beach popular with locals and visitors alike, host to music festivals in summer
budvaBody.svetiStefan: The iconic islet-hotel visible from the coastal road, with a public beach on one side
budvaBody.becici: A long stretch of fine sand that was once voted the most beautiful beach in Europe
budvaBody.oldTownText: Budva's Old Town (Stari Grad) is a compact walled citadel on a small peninsula. Inside the walls you'll find narrow cobblestone streets, Venetian-era architecture, small churches dating back to the 7th century, and a lively mix of restaurants, galleries, and boutiques. The Citadela fortress at the southern tip offers panoramic views of the coast and nearby islands.
budvaBody.svetiStefanTrip: Just 8 km south, Sveti Stefan is a photogenic fortified island village now housing a luxury hotel. The views from the road above are spectacular and free. The mainland beach is open to all.
budvaBody.kotorTrip: A 30-minute drive north through a dramatic mountain tunnel brings you to the Bay of Kotor and its UNESCO-listed Old Town. Continue to Perast for Our Lady of the Rocks.
budvaBody.lovcenTrip: Head inland to Lovćen National Park for the Njegoš Mausoleum, perched at 1,657 metres with views across four countries on a clear day. The winding road from the coast is an experience in itself.
budvaBody.gettingAroundText: While Budva's Old Town is best explored on foot, a rental car is essential for reaching the best beaches, nearby villages, and inland attractions. Parking is available near the Old Town walls and at several public lots along the main road. In peak summer, arriving early secures the best spots.

perastBody.p1: Perast is a charming baroque village on the Bay of Kotor, about 35 km from Budva (roughly 40 minutes by car). The drive takes you through Kotor and along the spectacular bay coastline.
perastBody.p2: Although home to only around 850 people, Perast's long and colourful history outweighs its small size. From 1420 to 1797, the town belonged to the Republic of Venice and was known for its master shipbuilders and sailors. The village has gorgeous examples of Baroque architecture.
perastBody.stGeorgeText: A natural island just off the coast in the Bay of Kotor, home to the St. George Benedictine Monastery, built in the 12th century. Small boats from the harbour will take visitors there for a few Euros.
perastBody.ladyOfRocksText: A man-made island holding the church of the same name. The island was created by sailors throwing rocks and sinking old boats over centuries. Every year this tradition continues, and on July 22nd tourists can watch as locals fill their boats with rocks to help keep the island intact.
perastBody.historicText: The town boasts numerous palaces, cathedrals, and several guard towers. The Museum of Perast contains many artefacts, archives, paintings, sculptures, and town records. The museum is located in the old Palata Buiovich, considered the most beautiful building on the Montenegrin coast.
perastBody.monasteryText: Located just north of Perast on the road to Risan, the Banja Monastery features many ancient religious pieces from Russian, Greek, and Boka dynasties.

tivatBody.p1: Tivat is home to Montenegro's coastal airport — Tivat Airport — making it the gateway for most visitors heading to Budva. The town itself is worth a stop, with a luxury marina, waterfront dining, and bay views.
tivatBody.p2: This seaside resort has a population of around 13,000. Visitors can enjoy seafront boardwalks, dine at waterfront bistros, and explore shops tucked into narrow alleyways. During peak season, the town balances tranquillity with vibrant tourism activity.
tivatBody.beachesText: Tivat boasts 17 beaches spanning 3 kilometres of coastline. Top beaches include:
tivatBody.beach1: Plavi Horizanti (Blue Horizons): White sand, crystal clear waters, perfect for swimming and sunbathing
tivatBody.beach2: Przno on Sveti Marko Island: An abandoned resort area offering seclusion and pristine waters
tivatBody.portoText: The jewel of Tivat is Porto Montenegro, a luxury yacht marina and waterfront village that has transformed the town into one of the Adriatic's most fashionable destinations. The marina complex features boutiques, restaurants, a pool club, and regular events throughout the summer.
tivatBody.monasteryText: 10th-century ruins located on the Prevlaka peninsula, with ongoing archaeological work uncovering the site's ancient history.
tivatBody.churchText: Built in 1734, this church contains religious paintings and artefacts, with beautiful views over the bay.
tivatBody.museumText: Just a 10-minute drive away in Kotor, the Maritime Museum displays Montenegro's rich seafaring heritage.

tivatAirportBody.p1: Tivat Airport is the closest airport to Budva — just 22 km away, about a 30-minute drive. The facility has undergone recent modernisation with a new terminal. The airport code is TIV.
tivatAirportBody.p2: Tivat Airport has more flights during the summer peak season but maintains daily flights to Belgrade year-round. Charter flights operate heavily during summer months.
tivatAirportBody.facilitiesText: The airport offers cafes, public telephones, souvenir stores, and duty-free shopping. Bus services connect to Herceg Novi and Budva, with taxi service also available outside the terminal.
tivatAirportBody.whyText: Tivat Airport is the closest airport to Budva and the Montenegrin Riviera. Within 30 minutes of landing, you can be in Budva's Old Town or on your way to Sveti Stefan. Picking up your car at Tivat means no transfers, no waiting for buses — just straight from the terminal to the riviera.
tivatAirportBody.meetText: We meet you at the arrivals hall with your name on a sign. Your car is parked right outside. Total time from landing to driving: about 10 minutes.

podgoricaBody.p1: Podgorica is Montenegro's capital and largest city, sitting 65 km inland from Budva (about 1 hour by car). Most tourists flying into Podgorica Airport head straight for the coast, but the capital is worth a stop.
podgoricaBody.p2: The city's architecture reflects diverse historical influences. Ottoman rule left its mark, while medieval structures stand alongside communist-era buildings and contemporary glass towers. The Millennium Bridge has become a symbol of the city's modern aesthetic.
podgoricaBody.p3: Podgorica is the cultural capital of the country. Those who rush off to Budva's beaches without spending time here will miss an important part of Montenegro.
podgoricaBody.seeText: Notable attractions include Sat-Kula (the Ottoman clock tower) and Duklja, an archaeological site dating back approximately two thousand years. Shopping highlights include Italian fabrics, art galleries, and local wines.
podgoricaBody.skadarText: Potentially Europe's most beautiful lake, located on the Montenegro-Albania border as a National Park. Home to millions of pelicans, it is the largest lake in the Balkans.
podgoricaBody.danilovgradText: A scenic river town approximately thirty minutes west of Podgorica, featuring riverside beaches, boat rides, galleries, and the nearby Zdrebaonik monastery.
podgoricaBody.zabljak: An alpine town in the Durmitor mountain region, sitting 1,500 metres above sea level and 170 kilometres from Podgorica. Skiing in winter, hiking and rafting in summer.

podgoricaAirportBody.p1: Podgorica Airport is Montenegro's largest airport, 65 km from Budva (about 1 hour by car). It has more international connections than Tivat and is a good option if Tivat flights are unavailable.
podgoricaAirportBody.p2: A new terminal opened in May 2006, while the old terminal now functions as a museum documenting Montenegro's aviation history. The airport code TGD references the city's former name, Titograd.
podgoricaAirportBody.distIntro: The airport is situated 12 kilometres south of Podgorica city centre.
podgoricaAirportBody.flightsText: The airport handles both international and domestic flights. Daily service connects to Belgrade, with regular flights to Paris, Rome, Frankfurt, Vienna, Budapest, Ljubljana, and Moscow.
podgoricaAirportBody.facilitiesText: The terminal features eight departure gates, two arrival gates, and amenities including cafes, duty-free shopping, and various services.
podgoricaAirportBody.awardText: Although small, Podgorica Airport has won the ACI Europe award for the best airport under 1 million passengers.
podgoricaAirportBody.rentalText: We meet you at the arrivals hall and bring the car to you. No queues, no shuttle buses. Within minutes of clearing customs, you'll be on the road heading to Budva and the riviera.

dubrovnikAirportBody.h1: Renting a Car at Dubrovnik Airport (DBV)
dubrovnikAirportBody.p1: Many visitors to Budva fly into Dubrovnik Airport in Croatia, which often has more international connections. The airport is roughly a 2.5-hour drive from Budva, depending on the border crossing.
dubrovnikAirportBody.p2: Since this is such a common route, crossing the border is straightforward — provided that visitors have all their rental agreements and travel documents in order.
dubrovnikAirportBody.p3: Renting a car at Dubrovnik and driving to Budva lets you see stunning Adriatic coastline along the way, with the option to stop in Herceg Novi and Kotor en route.
dubrovnikAirportBody.borderText: During tourist season, long waits to cross the Montenegro-Croatia border are not uncommon. Crossing early in the mornings or late at night helps beat the crowds. Make sure your rental company has provided a Green Card for cross-border insurance.
dubrovnikAirportBody.facilitiesText1: Dubrovnik Airport has a modern terminal building located less than half an hour's drive from Dubrovnik city on the E65. Parking is limited in high season — pre-book if possible.
dubrovnikAirportBody.facilitiesText2: Inside the airport, there is a bureau de change, bank, and post office. Restaurants, coffee shops, duty-free, and a newsstand are also available.
dubrovnikAirportBody.gettingText1: The drive from Dubrovnik Airport to Budva takes you along one of Europe's most scenic coastal roads. You'll pass through the border at Debeli Brijeg and continue along the Montenegrin coast.
dubrovnikAirportBody.gettingText2: Within 2.5 hours you'll pass through the Bay of Kotor and arrive in Budva — one of the most dramatic coastal drives in the Mediterranean.

montenegroBody.h1: Exploring Montenegro by Car from Budva
montenegroBody.p1: Montenegro stands as one of Europe's most rewarding destinations for self-drive exploration. From dramatic mountain roads and quiet inland villages to winding coastal drives along the Adriatic, the country offers diverse landscapes within surprisingly short distances. Renting a car in Budva puts you at the centre of it all.
montenegroBody.p2: Although compact in size, Montenegro requires longer driving times than maps suggest. Mountain switchbacks, narrow coastal roads, and scenic routes encourage frequent stops. Travellers gain the freedom to experience both journey and destination at their own pace.
montenegroBody.startText: Most visitors pick up their rental car at Tivat Airport (22 km from Budva) or Podgorica Airport (65 km). Dubrovnik Airport in Croatia remains popular for wider flight connections. From Budva, you're perfectly placed to explore the coast in both directions and head inland to the mountains.
montenegroBody.timesText1: Actual driving times regularly exceed what maps show. Mountain switchbacks, jagged coastline roads, and rural obstacles all contribute to longer journeys. The scenic experience more than justifies the time.
montenegroBody.timesText2: Sveti Stefan: 15 min. Kotor: 30 min. Tivat: 30 min. Petrovac: 20 min. Herceg Novi: 1 hr. Podgorica: 1 hr. Dubrovnik: 2.5 hrs.
montenegroBody.rulesIntro: Traffic police checks occur frequently but present no concern if you have proper documentation. You'll need:
montenegroBody.rule1: A valid driver's licence (international licence accepted)
montenegroBody.rule2: Rental contract or proof of vehicle ownership
montenegroBody.rule4: A "Green Card" for border crossings (approximately 15 Euros for 15 days)
montenegroBody.keyRule1: Seat belts are mandatory for all passengers
montenegroBody.keyRule2: Mobile phone use while driving is prohibited
montenegroBody.keyRule3: Any drinking and driving is illegal — zero tolerance
montenegroBody.conditionsText: City roads maintain fair condition. Mountain roads can be more challenging, with minimal guard rails in places. Some drivers operate aggressively, so caution is advised — particularly on mountain passes and narrow coastal stretches.
montenegroBody.e65Text: Head north from Budva through the tunnel to Kotor, then along the bay to Perast, Risan, and Herceg Novi. This is the most scenic route on the Montenegrin coast.
montenegroBody.e762Text: Head south from Budva to Petrovac, Bar, and Ulcinj. Or turn inland at Podgorica for the mountains — Durmitor, Žabljak, and the Tara Canyon.
montenegroBody.borderText: Cross-border travel is straightforward with the right documentation. Checkpoints can experience heavy congestion during peak season. Optimal crossing times are weekday mornings or late evenings.
montenegroBody.nationText: Montenegro achieved independence in June 2006, making it one of the world's newest nations. Tourism development continues to grow, with Budva leading the way as the country's premier resort destination.

borderCrossingBody.h1: Can I Take My Rental Car Across Borders?
borderCrossingBody.p1: Yes. Cross-border travel is permitted from Montenegro to Croatia, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Albania, Kosovo, and Serbia. You just need to let your rental company know at booking so they can prepare the necessary documentation.
borderCrossingBody.greenCardText: A Green Card is an international motor insurance certificate that proves your vehicle is insured in the country you're entering. It's required at every border crossing in the Balkans.
borderCrossingBody.greenCard2: Where to get it: your rental company arranges this — just confirm at booking
borderCrossingBody.greenCard3: Countries covered: Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, Kosovo, Serbia
borderCrossingBody.greenCardWarn: Without a Green Card, border guards will not let you through. Always check that the card covers all countries on your planned route before you set off.
borderCrossingBody.docsTitle: Documents You Need at the Border
borderCrossingBody.doc1: Valid passport or EU ID card
borderCrossingBody.doc5: Vehicle registration document (provided with the car)
borderCrossingBody.croatiaText: The most popular crossing for tourists. The main checkpoint is at Debeli Brijeg on the coastal road (E65) between Herceg Novi and Dubrovnik. This crossing gets extremely busy in summer — expect queues of 1-2 hours during peak season.
borderCrossingBody.croatiaTip: From Budva, allow 1 hour to reach the border at Debeli Brijeg. Best times to cross: early morning (before 8am) or late evening (after 8pm).
borderCrossingBody.croatiaFerry: There is also the Kamenari-Lepetane ferry shortcut across the Bay of Kotor which avoids driving around the entire bay — saves about 45 minutes.
borderCrossingBody.albaniaText: Two main crossings: Sukobin/Muriqan (coastal, near Ulcinj) and Hani i Hotit (inland, near Skadar Lake). The coastal crossing is more traveller-friendly.
borderCrossingBody.albaniaNote: Note: Albanian roads differ significantly from Montenegro. Drive cautiously, especially on rural roads.
borderCrossingBody.bosniaText: The main crossing is at Šćepan Polje (near Foča) if heading north, or via Vilusi from Nikšić. Crossings are generally quiet with minimal wait times.
borderCrossingBody.serbiaText: Cross at Dobrakovo (on the E65 north of Bijelo Polje). This crossing connects directly to the Serbian motorway system towards Belgrade.
borderCrossingBody.kosovoText: The crossing at Kula (near Rožaje) connects to Peja/Peć in Kosovo. Check with your rental company that Kosovo is included on your Green Card.
borderCrossingBody.tip1: Have all documents ready before you reach the checkpoint — don't search for them at the window
borderCrossingBody.tip2: Turn off your engine while waiting in queues (border guards appreciate it)
borderCrossingBody.tip3: Summer weekends are the worst — plan to cross on weekdays if possible
borderCrossingBody.tip4: The Croatian coastal crossing (Debeli Brijeg) is busiest; inland crossings are quieter
borderCrossingBody.tip5: Keep some cash in Euros — useful for tolls and minor purchases on the other side
borderCrossingBody.tip6: Mobile data roaming: check your plan covers the country you're entering
borderCrossingBody.lawsTitle: Driving Rules That Change at Borders
borderCrossingBody.lawsIntro: Each country has slightly different traffic laws. Key differences to note:
borderCrossingBody.law1: Croatia: headlights must be on at all times. Motorway tolls apply (cash or card)
borderCrossingBody.law2: Albania: speed limits are lower on rural roads. Road quality varies significantly
borderCrossingBody.law3: Bosnia: winter tyres mandatory November-April. Carry a first-aid kit and warning triangle
borderCrossingBody.law4: Serbia: zero alcohol tolerance for drivers. Motorway vignette required
borderCrossingBody.law5: Kosovo: drive on the right. International driving permit recommended
borderCrossingBody.route1: Budva → Dubrovnik: 2.5 hours via coastal E65. Stunning scenery, one border crossing
borderCrossingBody.route2: Budva → Mostar (Bosnia): 4 hours via Trebinje. Visit the famous bridge
borderCrossingBody.route3: Budva → Shkodër (Albania): 3 hours via Ulcinj coastal crossing
borderCrossingBody.route4: Budva → Belgrade (Serbia): 6 hours via E65 north through Podgorica
borderCrossingBody.ctaText: Need a car with cross-border documentation ready? Search our fleet — just select your destination countries at booking and we'll arrange the Green Card.
